theory.intro: Angle pairs are classified by their position or by the total of their measures.
facts: **Vertically opposite** angles lie across from each other when two lines intersect; they share only the vertex.
**Adjacent** angles share a vertex and one arm, with interiors that do not overlap.
**Complementary** angles have measures that add to $90^{\circ}$.
**Supplementary** angles have measures that add to $180^{\circ}$.
A pair can have more than one description; for example, adjacent angles can also be complementary.

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.75]\draw (-2,-1.4)--(2,1.4);\draw (-2,1.4)--(2,-1.4);\draw (0.45,0.32) arc[start angle=35,end angle=145,radius=0.55];\draw (-0.45,-0.32) arc[start angle=215,end angle=325,radius=0.55];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Identify the relationship between the two marked angles.
Which relationship describes angles measuring $32^{\circ}$ and $58^{\circ}$?
Which relationship describes angles measuring $117^{\circ}$ and $63^{\circ}$?

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\draw (O)--(2,0) node[right] {$A$};\draw (O)--(1.2,1.7) node[above] {$B$};\draw (O)--(-1.5,1.3) node[above] {$C$};\node[below] at (O) {$O$};\draw (0.5,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=55,radius=0.5];\draw (0.42,0.6) arc[start angle=55,end angle=139,radius=0.72];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Identify the relationship between $\angle AOB$ and $\angle BOC$.

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\draw (-2,-1.4) node[below] {$C$}--(2,1.4) node[above] {$A$};\draw (-2,1.4) node[above] {$B$}--(2,-1.4) node[below] {$D$};\node[below] at (O) {$O$};\draw (0.42,0.3) arc[start angle=35,end angle=145,radius=0.52];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which angle is vertically opposite $\angle AOB$?
From $24^{\circ}$, $66^{\circ}$ and $156^{\circ}$, choose the angle complementary to $24^{\circ}$.
From $42^{\circ}$, $138^{\circ}$ and $148^{\circ}$, choose the angle supplementary to $42^{\circ}$.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\foreach \a/\n in {10/A,70/B,155/C,240/D}{\draw (O)--(\a:2) node[anchor=180+\a] {$\n$};}\node[below] at (O) {$O$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Are $\angle AOB$ and $\angle COD$ adjacent? Give the positional reason.
development.solution_text: No. They share a vertex but do not share an arm.

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\draw (O)--(2,0) node[right] {$A$};\draw (O)--(35:2) node[above right] {$B$};\draw (O)--(90:2) node[above] {$C$};\node[below] at (O) {$O$};\draw (0.45,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=35,radius=0.45];\draw (35:0.65) arc[start angle=35,end angle=90,radius=0.65];\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Angles $\angle AOB$ and $\angle BOC$ measure $35^{\circ}$ and $55^{\circ}$. Give every relationship from this skill that describes the pair.
Two angles measure $70^{\circ}$ each. Which named relationships from this skill are guaranteed by the measures alone?
mastery.solution_text: None. They are neither complementary nor supplementary, and position is needed to decide adjacent or vertically opposite.
